text.title: pnpm-package-management Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for pnpm-package-management as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Setting shamefully-hoist=true as a quick fix for "module not found" errors.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Defeats pnpm's core value proposition. The real fix is adding the missing package as an explicit dependency. Hoisting hides the gap and spreads phantom imports to other consumers.

failure-mode.description: Mixing npm install or yarn add commands with pnpm in the same project.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Creates a second lockfile (package-lock.json or yarn.lock), corrupts node_modules layout, and produces non-reproducible installs. The preinstall hook with only-allow pnpm prevents this.

failure-mode.description: Using npx instead of pnpm dlx for one-shot package execution.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Works but bypasses pnpm's content-addressable store, creating a separate npm-managed cache and potentially using a different version than workspace deps.

failure-mode.description: Not committing pnpm-lock.yaml to source control.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without the lockfile, installs are not reproducible. Every install may resolve to different patch versions, silently changing behavior across environments.

failure-mode.description: LLMs paste npx instead of pnpm dlx — works but bypasses pnpm's content-addressable store.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use pnpm dlx for one-shot execution. This keeps all package resolution within pnpm's store and ensures version consistency with workspace dependencies.

failure-mode.description: auto-install-peers=true masks missing peer deps; agents over-rely on it then break in strict consumers.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Keep auto-install-peers=true for convenience but periodically audit peer dependencies manually. In published packages, explicit peer deps are required.

failure-mode.description: After pnpm patch, the agent commits only the patch file and forgets to commit the pnpm.patchedDependencies block in package.json.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Both the patch file and the package.json registration are required atomically. The patch file alone is ignored at install time.

failure-mode.description: Native add-ons (sharp, node-canvas) sometimes need node-linker=hoisted for tools that scan node_modules flat.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use node-linker=hoisted in .npmrc for that specific project, document the reason, and link to the upstream issue tracking the fix.

failure-mode.description: Some Jest/Webpack plugins assume hoisted layout — strict mode breaks them; agents then add shamefully-hoist=true defeating the point.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Investigate the failing plugin first. The real fix is usually adding a missing peer dep or using the node-linker=hoisted exception with documentation.

failure-mode.description: pnpm patch workflow is great but agents forget pnpm patch-commit and lose the diff.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: After pnpm patch, always run pnpm patch-commit immediately. Commit both the patch file and the package.json pnpm.patchedDependencies block in the same commit.

failure-mode.description: Lockfile churn between pnpm major versions without pinned packageManager.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Pin via "packageManager": "pnpm@9.x.y" in root package.json. Corepack enforces the same version everywhere, eliminating lockfile-format drift.

failure-mode.description: workspace:* protocol doesn't work with npm publish — must use pnpm publish which rewrites it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Always publish workspace packages with pnpm publish or pnpm -r publish. Never use npm publish for packages with workspace:* dependencies.
